subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV 2026, World Bank, Freedom House, RSF, HRW, Amnesty, ITUC, CRS/Atlantic Council); Qatari state/state-aligned media (incl. Al Jazeera on Qatar's own governance) EXCLUDED there, with economic figures admitted only via IMF validation.

Migrant-labor reality: non-citizen workers are ~88-91% of the population under a kafala-legacy system; citizens are a ~10-12% minority. This structurally shapes demographics, cohesion, and rights metrics.
Standout features: immense fiscal strength, stability, safety, and geopolitical centrality, against an absolute-monarchy rights ceiling and a long-term water/heat tail.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May - the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: Among the world's strongest fiscal positions and sound banks, with a peg that surrenders monetary autonomy for stability.

fiscal_state.notes: A massive LNG-revenue base, but the Hormuz blockade cuts the near-term cash flow that funds the budget and sovereign-fund inflows. (IMF; Reuters)

monetary_independence.notes: A hard USD peg imports US policy with near-zero autonomy, but the peg plus gas wealth deliver stability. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Peg stability backed by a large sovereign fund; not a reserve issuer. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised (a capital ratio ~20%) with a stable outlook. (IMF; Moody's)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Qatari LNG - among the world's largest - ships almost entirely through the Strait of Hormuz with no pipeline bypass; the closure freezes the export economy near-term, recovering on reopening. (Reuters; BBC)

institutional.notes: High wealth-enabled state capacity and functional commercial law, against no political-sphere judicial independence and the absence of independent media.

rule_of_law.notes: An absolute monarchy with no judicial independence on political matters, but functional commercial law and low corruption. (Freedom House; V-Dem)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic statistics are IMF-validated; political data are opaque. (IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Low - no domestic independent media on Qatar's own affairs. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A wealthy, stable monarchy, against no political rights and a migrant-majority, two-tier social structure.

political_stability.notes: A wealthy, stable absolute monarchy near-term, with long-term gas-transition and succession caution. (Freedom House; IMF)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'Not Free' - no political rights, restricted expression, and same-sex relations criminalised. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Easy employer-sponsored residency and no income tax, but a migrant-majority workforce under kafala-legacy controls with no path to citizenship. (HRW; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: Orderly with very low crime, but a citizen-minority/migrant-majority two-tier structure. (HRW)

demographics.notes: Migrant-skewed, with a small young citizen base. (UN; HRW)

geopolitical.notes: A well-connected US partner and regional mediator with an open capital account, against regional turbulence exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: Hosts the largest US base in the region and is a major non-NATO ally and pivotal Gaza/Afghanistan mediator - courted and well-connected. (CRS; Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: Hosts the US Al Udeid air base; exposed to Iranian retaliation in the 2026 war. (Al Jazeera)

physical_practical.notes: World-class infrastructure, healthcare, and safety, against extreme heat and water scarcity long-term.

climate.notes: Extreme/near-unliveable wet-bulb heat by mid-century, desal-dependent water, coastal exposure, with very high wealth/AC offset. (IPCC AR6; WB)

infrastructure.notes: Top-tier infrastructure whose desalination system is, per analysts, 'huge, expertly engineered, and highly vulnerable' - and almost all drinking water depends on it. (Circle of Blue; CSIS)

crime_safety.notes: Very low crime - a clear strength. (independent)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low natural-catastrophe exposure besides heat and coastal flooding. (UN)

summary: One of the world's strongest fiscal positions - twin surpluses, low debt, vast LNG revenue, a large sovereign fund, and an LNG expansion - with sound banks, low inflation, world-class infrastructure and healthcare, very low crime, and high geopolitical centrality as a major US partner and regional mediator. The offsets are an absolute-monarchy rights ceiling (no political rights, restricted expression, criminalised same-sex relations), a migrant-majority workforce under kafala-legacy controls with no path to citizenship, a USD peg that surrenders monetary autonomy for stability, and a long-term extreme-heat and water-scarcity tail.
